review_execution.command: claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools "" --output-format json < staged diff prompt > /tmp/cp00-111-claude-review-output-valid.json

findings.title: Audit-hint preview-only property computed but never asserted
findings.evidence: runPermissionKernelCp111SyntheticFixtureProfile computes audit_hint_preview_only = workflow.audit_hint_preview?.emitted_to_audit_ledger === false, but neither validatePermissionKernelCp111Coverage nor the new tests assert it is true for the audit_hint_preview profile. That profile's scenario is identical to a plain allow (allowRule, no audit-specific input), so the explicit 'audit hint preview only' scope item is verified only indirectly via writes_audit_event === false, not via the dedicated preview-only flag.
findings.recommendation: Assert in validatePermissionKernelCp111Coverage and the test that the audit_hint_preview profile yields audit_hint_preview_only === true, so a regression that emits the hint to the audit ledger is caught directly rather than relying on the persistence-boundary flag.

findings.title: HRX embedded-only boundary value not asserted
findings.evidence: createPermissionKernelCp111CloseoutHandoff hardcodes hrx_embedded_boundary = 'embedded_people_hr_evidence_module_inside_law_firm_os' (correct), but the new handoff test and scripts/validate-rp02-permission-kernel-contract.mjs only check ldip_implemented === false plus next_pack_id/next_subphase_id; the HRX-embedded value is unverified.
findings.recommendation: Add an assertion on handoff.hrx_embedded_boundary to lock the embedded-only HRX boundary named in scope.

findings.title: Fail-closed/metadata-only semantics inherited from out-of-diff CP00-110 workflow
findings.evidence: All asserted statuses (blocked_before_permission_evaluation, blocked_claim_output, completed_metadata_only, review_required_routing), decision_reason object_acl_allow, and the no-write flags are produced by executePermissionKernelCp110Workflow in service-workflow.js, which is not in this diff. The profile builder uses direct property access (workflow.decision.effect, workflow.idempotency_receipt.persisted, workflow.lock_receipt.acquired, workflow.persistence_boundary.*) for the precheck-blocked profiles, assuming the upstream workflow always returns a fully-populated envelope; only audit_hint_preview uses optional chaining.
findings.recommendation: No change required for closeout: the runtime validator and tests assert the observable outputs of every profile. Recorded for traceability — the boundary guarantees rest on the CP00-110 workflow contract, not on code introduced in this pack.

parsed_review.recommendation: Approve CP00-111 closeout. The pack cleanly covers the planned Risk A range RP02.P02.M06.S03-S12 (10 units, first/last asserted), fail-closed tenant/matter prechecks that block before evaluator invocation, permission allow/deny and object-ACL allow paths, state-transition review routing, and metadata-only idempotency/lock/persistence receipts. The no-write attestation (15 flags incl. AI/export/external-share/LDIP) is comprehensive and cross-checked in both the JS source contract and the JSON contract; metadata-only behavior is verified across all 10 profiles; handoff is fixed to CP00-112/RP02.P02.M06.S13 and HRX stays embedded. The P2/P3 items are non-blocking coverage gaps and can be folded into CP00-112.
parsed_review.rationale: The diff adds a synthetic-only fixture-boundary layer over the established CP00-110 workflow with no product-state, audit, database, share/export, AI, or LDIP implementation. README, JS source contract, and JSON contract are mutually consistent, and the validator enforces required/fail-closed/metadata-only profile lists plus the no-write attestation on both sides. Fail-closed semantics are correctly distinguished (precheck blocks evaluator_invoked=false vs. permission_deny evaluator_invoked=true then blocked_claim_output) and tested. No security or correctness defects were found; the only gaps are an unasserted audit-preview flag and an unasserted HRX value, neither of which weakens the verified no-write safety invariant.
notes: No P0/P1 findings were reported by the valid CP00-111 pack-level Claude review.
Original P2 finding about audit hint preview-only assertion was fixed by tests and validator/source coverage hardening.
P3 finding about HRX embedded boundary assertion was fixed by tests and validator coverage hardening.
P3 finding about CP00-110 inheritance was documented as non-blocking; CP111 asserts observable profile outputs and CP00-110 remains a closed upstream pack.
No second valid Claude review was run.
